message:
fix(fresh-eyes audit): promote_with_instance semantics + naming + docs

Fresh-eyes review of the work landed in this session's 3-epic sprint
surfaced four concrete bugs. All fixed + regression-tested.

## Bug A (critical) — promote_with_instance rejected instance-agnostic tokens

Symptom: `CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>::promote_with_instance(id)`
returned `MissingField("instance_id")` when the token had no
`INSTANCE_ID` claim. But the LEGACY
`CapabilityVerifier::new(instance_id).verify_bound(...)` path ACCEPTS
such tokens (verify_claims_inner only enforces the instance check
when the claim is present — an absent claim means the token is
instance-agnostic / universally applicable).

This broke the two-phase equivalence property the jkcka epic
stands on: if one path rejects where the other accepts, the
type-level safety guarantee is unsound.

Fix (capability.rs): promote_with_instance now matches verify_bound
semantics — an instance-agnostic token (no INSTANCE_ID claim)
promotes unconditionally.

Added 2 regression tests:
- promote_with_instance_succeeds_for_instance_agnostic_token
- promote_with_instance_equivalence_to_direct_bound_verify (property
  test: both paths agree for instance-agnostic tokens)

## Bug B (minor) — ZoneViolation error had empty zone fields

`promote_with_instance` on a mismatched instance returned
`FcpError::ZoneViolation { source_zone: "", target_zone: "", ... }`.
The existing verify_claims_inner code for the same error populates
the zone fields from the claim. Fix: extract zone_id from the
claims and populate both fields symmetrically.

The existing `promote_with_instance_wrong_id_returns_error` test
was upgraded to assert the zone fields are populated correctly.

## Bug C (API surface) — claims_unbound() vs claims() asymmetry

`CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>::claims()` and
`CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>::claims_unbound()` had asymmetric
names. Since they're on different concrete types, there's no method
resolution conflict — both can be `claims()`. The
`claims_unbound` name created a caller footgun (it LOOKS like a
variant that skips checks).

Fix: rename `claims_unbound` → `claims`. Updated the one production
call site in fcp-host/bin/fcp-host.rs and the two in-tree test
usages.

## Bug D (docstring inaccuracy) — Documented the bug

The `promote_with_instance` docstring claimed "Returns MissingField
if the token has no instance_id claim." This documented Bug A as
intended behavior. Rewrote the docstring to describe the correct
three-way semantics (match / mismatch / absent-claim).

## Clippy cleanup

Fresh-eyes pass also surfaced clippy lints in my new code:
- fcp-auth-schema/src/lib.rs — missing backticks on COSE_Sign1
- fcp-auth-schema/src/claims.rs — missing backticks on DateTime;
  value_kind could be `const fn` (applied)
- fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs (synthesize_grants) — 3 missing-backtick
  warnings on GRANTS/OPERATIONS/MissingField references
- fcp-core/src/capability.rs — CryptographicallyVerified comment
  missing backticks; `claims()` methods on BoundVerified +
  UnboundVerified need `# Panics` docs + the
  `missing_const_for_fn` allow mirroring the legacy impl pattern

All landed; fcp-auth-schema + fcp-core/cose portions now clippy-clean.

## Verification

- fcp-auth-schema + fcp-crypto lib: 536 green
- fcp-core lib: 3951 green (up from 3949 — +2 new regression tests)
- fcp-host gateway-handoff E2E: 3 green
- fcp-conformance auth_claims: 5 green
- fcp-protocol session: 13 green (including crkft suite)
- trybuild compile-fail: 1 passing
- Cross-crate: 4508 total tests green

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
